digest.component_or_area: rebar live-store fsck / event-store snapshot integrity
propositions: rebar fsck reported ORPHAN_EVENT and MISSING_CREATE faults against the live store, questioned as either binary/store version skew or genuine faults.
The faults persist under the current ENQUEUE_ENRICH-era binary (ensures 5/5 applied, no newer-than-binary warning), proving they are real and not version-skew false positives.

ORPHAN_EVENTs are benign snapshot-staleness from a compaction-vs-reconciler write race where a compaction snapshots before observing a reconciler EDIT that is later re-emitted; live status/assignee remain correct.
MISSING_CREATEs are local-only phantom dirs with no tracked files or HEAD references, one being a duplicate of an already-tracked epic, so no content is lost.
Remediation must use A3-safe tooling (retire orphan EDIT files to .json.retired and delete phantom dirs), not fsck --repair-snapshots which previously corrupted statuses.
After remediation rebar fsck reports zero ORPHAN_EVENT and zero MISSING_CREATE with all orphan tickets' status and assignee byte-identical before and after.
